title: RRB NTPC 2026
meta_description: RRB NTPC 2026 — Non-Technical Popular Categories recruitment in Indian Railways. Graduation and 12th pass posts, salary ₹19-35K, exam pattern, and preparation guide.

hero.one_liner: Railway recruitment for Non-Technical Popular Categories — Station Master, Goods Guard, Clerk, and other posts across Indian Railways

what_is_it: RRB NTPC (Railway Recruitment Board — Non-Technical Popular Categories) is a large-scale recruitment for various non-technical posts in Indian Railways. The exam recruits for both graduate-level and 12th-pass level posts. Graduate posts include Station Master, Goods Guard, Commercial Apprentice, Senior Clerk, and Junior Account Assistant. 12th-pass posts include Trains Clerk, Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerk, and Junior Clerk.

The exam is conducted by 21 Railway Recruitment Boards across India. The selection process has multiple stages: CBT-1 (Computer Based Test — screening), CBT-2 (merit), Typing Skill Test (for specific posts), and Document Verification. The difficulty level is moderate — between SSC and banking exams.

Indian Railways is India's largest employer with over 12 lakh employees. Railway jobs offer excellent benefits — free rail travel (lifetime pass for employee + family), medical (including family), quarters/HRA, pension (NPS), annual increments, and job security. The work-life balance varies by post — Station Masters work in shifts, while Clerks have regular office hours.
RRB NTPC 2026 is expected to follow a new notification cycle. Check the official RRB website for your zone's specific notification date and vacancy details.

key_details.label: Conducting Body
key_details.value: Railway Recruitment Boards (21 RRBs across India)

key_details.label: Education (Graduate posts)
key_details.value: Bachelor's degree from recognized university — for Station Master, Goods Guard, Commercial Apprentice, etc.

key_details.label: Education (12th pass posts)
key_details.value: 12th pass (10+2) — for Trains Clerk, Ticket Clerk, Junior Clerk

key_details.label: Age Limit
key_details.value: 18-33 years (General) for graduate posts. 18-30 for 12th pass posts. Relaxation: OBC +3, SC/ST +5.

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: CBT-1 (screening) → CBT-2 (merit) → Typing Test (for specific posts) → Document Verification → Medical

exam_pattern.description: Online exam with 100 MCQs. This is a qualifying round — 3× candidates are shortlisted for CBT-2. CBT-1 marks are NOT counted in final merit.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: 1/3rd of marks deducted per wrong answer

exam_pattern.description: Determines final ranking. Same subjects but higher difficulty level. Separate CBT-2 for graduate and 12th-pass posts.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: 1/3rd of marks deducted per wrong answer

extra_sections.heading: Railway Job Perks — Beyond Salary
content: Free rail travel: This is the biggest perk. Railway employees get free passes for themselves, spouse, and dependent children — unlimited AC travel across India. After retirement, you get a lifetime pass. This alone saves lakhs over a career.
Medical: Free medical treatment at Railway hospitals for employee and entire family. Railway medical facilities are among the best government hospitals in India.
Quarters: Government accommodation is provided at nominal rent. If not available, HRA is paid (8-24% of basic depending on city category).
Other benefits: 8 Casual Leaves + 30 Earned Leaves per year, LTC (Leave Travel Concession), Children's Education Allowance, Group Insurance Scheme, and pension under NPS.
The total value of railway perks (free travel, medical, housing) is estimated at ₹3-5 lakh per year beyond the salary — making the effective compensation much higher than the stated salary.

preparation_tips: RRB NTPC syllabus overlaps significantly with SSC exams. If you're preparing for SSC CGL/CHSL, you can appear for NTPC with minimal additional preparation. Focus on General Awareness — it carries the highest weightage (40% in CBT-1).

Railway-specific GK is important: learn about Indian Railways history, zones, headquarters, recent initiatives (Vande Bharat, Kavach, bullet train project), railway budget highlights, and key appointments. 5-8 questions typically come from railway-specific knowledge.

Mathematics is at 10th-12th class level — not as difficult as banking or SSC CGL. Focus on: percentage, ratio, profit-loss, SI/CI, time-work-distance, algebra basics, geometry, and data interpretation. Speed matters — practice with a timer.
Previous year RRB NTPC papers (2015, 2019-21 cycles) are the best preparation resource. The question patterns repeat frequently.

faqs.question: Is NTPC only for graduates?
faqs.answer: No. NTPC has both graduate-level and 12th-pass level posts. Graduate posts (Station Master, Goods Guard, Commercial Apprentice) require a Bachelor's degree. 12th-pass posts (Trains Clerk, Ticket Clerk, Junior Clerk) require only 10+2. Both are recruited through the same exam but with different cutoffs.

faqs.question: Station Master or Goods Guard — which is better?
faqs.answer: Both are at Pay Level 5-6 with similar salary. Station Master has a more prestigious and responsible role (managing an entire railway station) but involves shift duties. Goods Guard accompanies freight trains and involves travel. Station Master is generally considered the more desirable post. Choose based on your preference for office-based vs travel-based work.

faqs.question: Can I choose my posting zone?
faqs.answer: You apply to a specific RRB zone (based on your choice or home location). Your posting will be within that zone's jurisdiction. However, transfers between zones are possible after a few years of service. Apply to the zone where you want to be posted initially.

faqs.question: What is the typing test in RRB NTPC?
faqs.answer: Typing test is required only for specific posts like Senior Clerk, Junior Account Assistant, and some Commercial posts. Speed required: 30 wpm in English or 25 wpm in Hindi. It's qualifying, not merit-based. Station Master and Goods Guard posts do NOT require typing test.

faqs.question: How competitive is RRB NTPC?
faqs.answer: Very competitive — typically 1-2 crore applications for 10,000-30,000 vacancies. However, many applicants don't appear for the exam, and the actual competition is among 50-60 lakh candidates who take CBT-1. With 4-6 months of focused preparation, clearing CBT-1 cutoff is achievable for a dedicated aspirant.
